body.para: Software Verify (Software Verify Limited) makes a family of C/C++ analysis tools for Windows; the one used in EmulatR development is Performance Validator, a sampling and instrumenting profiler. It is the tool used to find where the emulator spends its time so optimization effort lands on the code that matters.

Performance Validator attributes run time to functions and lines and presents call graphs and call trees, so a hot path is visible rather than guessed. For an instruction-set emulator this is essential: a small number of routines dominate execution, and a few percent shaved off the inner loop is worth more than any amount of tuning elsewhere.

Profiling confirmed that the pipeline driver's per-instruction step() is the overwhelming majority of run time (on the order of 82 percent), which is why that loop ships optimized and the -O0 debug build is gated behind a separate CMake option. It also quantified the cold-boot decompression spin and the bounded firmware wait loops -- the measurements behind the performance items elsewhere in this documentation. The profiler is a developer tool only; it is not involved in running the emulator.
